cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out why Ms. Leibfried asked the author to recite a passage in front of the class. Let me go through the passage again to get the context. The author mentions that she was an introverted, shy student, the only non-white student, and felt out of place. She found solace in reading and writing. Ms. Leibfried noticed her interest and offered her favorite novels like "The Bell Jar." The big project was a book report, but Ms. Leibfried suggested the author do something different. The author chose a passage from "The Bell Jar" to recite. After the recital, the class applauded, and Ms. Leibfried praised her for picking a sensitive piece and delivering it beautifully. This made the author feel confident for the first time. The question is about why Ms. Leibfried asked her to recite. The options are A to D. Let's look at the options again: A) To test memory. B) To make her earn respect. C) To deepen understanding of the book. D) To guide her to build relationships. The key here is to understand Ms. Leibfried's intention. The author was shy and struggled with being teased. The teacher noticed her interest in reading and writing, made her feel less shy, and even gave her book suggestions. The project was a book report, but the teacher suggested something different. The author chose a passage to recite. Afterward, the teacher praised her for the sensitive choice and delivery. Why would the teacher suggest this? The main point seems to be about helping the author overcome her shyness. The act of reciting
